images.caption: Figure 1:Overview of GAE performance.Left:GAE establishes a superior Pareto curve between linear probing accuracy and latent dimension.Right:GAE with 32 latent dimension significantly accelerates convergence and delivers superior generation results at both 80 and 800 training epochs.

Figure 2:Overview of the GAE architecture. The input image is processed through a pixel-level branch (EpE_{p},ApA_{p}) and a frozen semantic branch (VFM,Es​pE_{sp}). A Semantic Preservation lossLs​pL_{sp}aligns the latent mean with the features from the decoupled Semantic Teacher.

Figure 3:Illustration of the three latent alignment paradigms: The termLs​pL_{sp}aligns the AE representations with those of the VFM.Pre Alignmentaligns high-dimensional encoder features directly;Post Alignmentprojects AE latents back to a high-dimensional space for supervision;Latent Alignmentoperates within the compressed latent bottleneck via a projection of VFM features.

Figure 4:Decoder stability against latent noise injection. We evaluate rFID by adding varying levels of Gaussian noise to the latent representations before decoding. The results demonstrate that models trained with higherσ\sigmaexhibit superior tolerance to latent distribution shifts, ensuring stable performance during generative sampling.
Figure 5:Qualitative results.Samples are generated by GAE (d=32d=32) after 800 epochs of training. A Classifier-Free Guidance (CFG) scale ofw=3.3w=3.3is utilized to enhance visual fidelity.
